name.marathi: इंदिरा गांधी राष्ट्रीय विधवा पेन्शन योजना
name.english: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme

scheme.launched_by: Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India
scheme.helpline: Varies by state (applications handled at local offices)

budget_provision.central_assistance: ₹300 per month for widows aged 40–79 years
budget_provision.state_additional_support: Varies by state (e.g., Maharashtra adds ₹1200 under Sanjay Gandhi Niradhar Anudan Yojana)
budget_provision.funding_source: Shared between Central Government and State Governments under NSAP framework

objective.primary: To provide financial assistance and social security to widows belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families
secondary: Ensure minimum income support for widows in vulnerable households
Promote dignity, independence, and social inclusion of widows
Reduce poverty and dependency among widowed women
Provide targeted support to widows in rural and urban areas

pension_assistance.note: States may provide additional top-up pension support

additional_benefits: State governments may provide top-up pension (e.g., Maharashtra adds ₹1200)
Eligibility for other NSAP-linked schemes such as old-age pension and disability pension
Access to UMANG app for digital application and tracking
Integration with Aadhaar for transparency and reduced leakages
Awareness programs to ensure inclusion of widows in rural and urban areas

residency.details: Applicant must be a resident of India

marital_status.details: Applicant must be a widow (husband deceased)

economic_status.details: Must belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) household

bank_account.details: Active savings bank account with Aadhaar linkage for DBT

required_documents: BPL proof (ration card or income certificate)
